items.correct: A prestige accent associated historically with public schools and the BBC — spoken by a small minority of Britons; it is an accent, not a dialect.
items.misconception: RP is the correct way to speak English.
items.random_wrong: RP is the accent everyone in southern England uses.
items.why: RP has high social prestige but no inherent correctness. Descriptive linguistics treats it as one accent among many — students absorb the prescriptive notion of 'correct' speech.

items.q: What is the difference between voiced and voiceless sounds?
items.correct: Voiced sounds are produced with vocal-fold vibration (/b/, /d/, /g/, /z/); voiceless sounds without it (/p/, /t/, /k/, /s/). You can feel the difference by putting a hand on your throat.
items.misconception: Voiced sounds are loud and voiceless sounds are whispered.
items.random_wrong: Voiced sounds use the lips; voiceless sounds use the tongue.
items.why: Voicing is about vocal-fold vibration, not volume. Whisper has no voicing at all but isn't silent. Students confuse phonation with loudness, missing how voicing distinguishes minimal pairs (pin/bin).

items.q: What is the difference between inflectional and derivational morphology?
items.correct: Inflectional morphemes mark grammatical features without changing word class (cat/cats, walk/walked); derivational morphemes create new words, often changing word class (happy → happiness, teach → teacher).
items.misconception: Inflection and derivation are two words for the same process.
items.random_wrong: Inflectional morphology applies only to verbs and derivational only to nouns.
items.why: The difference matters for word formation rules. English has very limited inflection but rich derivation. Students conflate the two, missing how morphology builds vocabulary vs marks grammar.

items.q: What does the L in PEEL stand for?
items.why: Correct answer: Link. “List” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which reading skill hunts specific facts?
items.why: Correct answer: Scanning. “Skimming” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many letters in PEEL paragraph structure?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 letters. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
